content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 20, 1990


Helen M. Kanowsky
City Clerk
City of West Sacramento
P.O. Box 966
West Sacramento, CA  95691

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. I-90-639

Dear Ms. Kanowsky:

     You have requested assistance regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/  The City of West Sacramento is planning to update its conflict of interest code in the near future.  You are asking if we could review the code and suggest any changes or revisions we may be necessary.  Since your request is for general assistance, we are treating it as a request for informal assistance.2/

     You should understand that since we have no knowledge of the duties and responsibilities of the persons designated, we cannot determine if the assigned disclosure is appropriate or that all persons within the city who are in decision-making capacities are covered by the code.

     I have itemized the areas of concern or needed revisions in the order of their appearance in the code.

1.  The Resolution appropriately incorporates the provisions of Commission Regulation 18730 as the standard terms of the code.  The reference to that regulation, 2 California Administrative Code Section 18730, was recently changed to 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18730.  That reference needs to be corrected in the second paragraph of the resolution.


1/    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/    Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.  (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Page Two
December 20, 1990


     2. The last paragraph of the resolution needs to state where
the statements of economic interests are to be filed. If the
place of filing is with the city clerk, then the following
paragraph would suffice: "All designated employees shall file
their statements of economic interests with the City Clerk.

     3. In the opening paragraph of Item I, the list of
designated positions, it is suggested that the second sentence be
revised to read: "The designated positions listed shall disclose
their economic interests in accordance with the corresponding
disclosure categories..." rather than "interests and investments."

     4. All designated positions have been assigned category 1,
and depending on the position, categories 2 through 4 may have
been assigned as well. Category 1 however is an all inclusive
category which requires the disclosure of all financial interests
within the City of West Sacramento. For all positions assigned
category 1, the assignment of additional categories is
unnecessary. Any interests disclosed under categories 2 through 4
is already required to be disclosed under Category 1.

     5. There is an asterisk behind the subheading Designated
Position*. This asterisk brings the reader to a footnote which
advises the reader that the term "Designated Position" includes
any other positions as designated by the City Manager. We have a
concern with this statement. As it reads it appears that this
gives the city manager the discretion to add any position at any
time.

     Government Code Section 87311 provides that the review and
preparation of codes by local government agencies shall be carried
out under procedures which guarantee to officers, employees,
members, and consultants of the agency and to residents of the
jurisdiction adequate notice and a fair opportunity to present
their views. Further, any changes to a code must be approved by
the city council before those changes become effective (Section
87303). We do not believe the city manager may, at anytime, add
positions to the code without those persons being given "adequate
notice and a fair opportunity to present their views." Further,
any amendments to the code must be approved by the city council.
It is suggested that this asterisk and its footnote be deleted.

     6. The list of designated positions includes the city
attorney. Section 87200 of the Act governs the disclosure and
disqualification obligations for city attorneys. This position
should be deleted from the conflict of interest code.

     7. Your code does not provide for the disclosure of economic
interests by consultants for the agency. The definition of a
designated employee includes consultants. (Section 82019.) A
consultant is any natural person who provides under contract,
information, advice, recommendation or counsel to any agency.
(Regulation 18700.)

Page Three
December 20, 1990


The Commission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the making of decisions on behalf of public agencies. Rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-making capacity, the Commission urges the use of a
specialized disclosure category that provides that the disclosure
required of consultants shall be determined on a case-by-case
basis by the city manager or city attorney. The city manager or
city attorney shall make a determination as to what disclosure, if
any, is required by any particular consultant. A copy of this
consultant disclosure category is enclosed, and should be
incorporated into your code.

8. With the disclosure categories themselves, it is
suggested that the monetary thresholds in each be deleted. The
monetary thresholds are set out in the model provisions of the
code (Regulation 18730). Should a legislative amendment increase
or decrease the monetary threshold for reporting interests, the
disclosure category portion of your code would be out of date and
in need of amendment. The monetary thresholds are set out in the
model code as well as the disclosure forms themselves.

The amount of verbiage in each category could be reduced by
rewriting each category. For instance, category 1 could read:
"All investments and business positions in business entities,
sources of income and interests in real property."

Category 2, if you continue to use it, is a little confusing
in its present format. It appears that you are asking for
investments in business entities which have an interest in real
property in the city or which are of the type to contract with the
city. Any time you are asking for an investment interest to be
disclosed, you should also require the corresponding business
positions and income from those sources as well. Category 2
should be rewritten to read: "Investments and business positions
in business entities, and sources of income from entities which
have an interest in real property within the City or entities
which are of the type to do business with the City." You will
note that I have deleted the references to "doing business during
the year previous to the date of disclosure" as well as to the
reference to entities which "are likely to do business with the
City." An investment is reportable if it is doing business or has
done business in a jurisdiction during the two years prior to the
time any statement is filed (Section 82034). The reference to
only disclosing entities if the employees believes is "likely" to
do business with the City places a judgment call on the employee.
If an entity has a two-year track history of doing business with
the city, it should be disclosed, regardless of whether the
employee believes it could do business in the subsequent year.

Category 3 needs to have business position disclosure added
to it and should read: "Investments and business positions in
business entities, interests in real property and sources of
income subject to the regulatory..."

Page Four
December 20, 1990


Category 4 also needs business position disclosure added and
would read: "Investments and business positions in business
entities, and sources of income which engage in land
development..."

I hope this has been helpful to you. If you need further
information or clarification, please feel free to give our office
a call at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

SH/JET/jt

sections.question_synthetic: The City of West Sacramento requests FPPC assistance in reviewing and updating its conflict of interest code, including suggested changes or revisions needed to ensure compliance with the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides eight specific areas of concern and recommended revisions to the City's conflict of interest code, including correcting regulatory references, clarifying filing locations for statements of economic interests, revising disclosure category language, removing unnecessary category overlaps, deleting the city manager's unilateral position designation authority, removing the city attorney position, adding consultant disclosure provisions, and eliminating monetary thresholds from category descriptions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with no explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers; question and conclusion information extracted from body text. Document provides detailed itemized feedback rather than a single conclusory statement. Footnotes provide important context regarding informal vs. formal advice and statutory/regulatory references.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The City of West Sacramento requests FPPC assistance in reviewing and updating its conflict of interest code, including suggested changes or revisions needed to ensure compliance with the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides eight specific areas of concern and recommended revisions to the City's conflict of interest code, including correcting regulatory references, clarifying filing locations for statements of economic interests, revising disclosure category language, removing unnecessary category overlaps, deleting the city manager's unilateral position designation authority, removing the city attorney position, adding consultant disclosure provisions, and eliminating monetary thresholds from category descriptions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the City of West Sacramento's request for informal assistance in updating its conflict of interest code by providing detailed feedback on eight substantive areas requiring revision, including regulatory citations, disclosure procedures, position designations, category assignments, and consultant provisions.
